•Install, configure, upgrade, and troubleshoot Cisco ASA firewalls (5500-X), including single and multi-context fire-
walls.

issues.

•Prepare design documents (HLD, LLD), IP Schema, and configurations for network devices as per customer require-
ments in data center environments.

balancers, and end-to-end coordination for firewall deployment.
•Utilized Cisco Security Manager 4.x.
Network Security Analyst Full-time
Zensar Technologies Egypt May 2017 - April 2020, Giza, Egypt

EMEA regions.
•Configure, upgrade, and troubleshoot Cisco Routers (29xx, 28xx, 19xx), Cisco Switches (2960, 3560), and Cisco Meraki
AP in Meraki Cloud.
•Perform F5 LTM configuration and troubleshoot operational issues.

problems with minimal downtime.

•Proficient in researching traffic patterns to identify false-positives and/or malicious traffic within IDS, IPS, scanners
(Qualys), and firewalls.

•Exposed to Change Configuration Management.
Network Administrator Full-time
Wipro Technolog

ies Egypt January 2010 - April 2011, Helwan, Egypt

forms (Cisco Router 3845, Cisco stack-wise switches 3750E and 2960 access switch, Cisco 4500 chassis, Cisco 5550/20
ASA firewalls, Juniper SA 6500 SSL VPN).

(Juniper SSL VPN) - Cisco Meraki AP

Management) - HLD - LLD

Ashraf Sayed is a Senior Network Security Analyst with contact information including email, phone number, GitHub, LinkedIn, and personal website.
Ashraf Sayed received a Bachelor of Engineering in Information Technology from Mumbai University between January 2006 and January 2010, located in Mumbai, Maharashtra.

From May 2020 to the present, Ashraf Sayed has worked as a Senior Network Security Analyst at Accenture Egypt in Cairo, Egypt, where he manages Cisco ASA firewalls, F5 LTM, Pulse Secure, PAC files, and prepares design documents.

From January 2010 to April 2011, the individual administered, managed, and troubleshooted a multi-vendor network infrastructure in Helwan, Egypt, including Cisco and Juniper platforms such as Cisco Router 3845, Cisco stack-wise switches 3750E and 2960, Cisco 4500 chassis, Cisco 5550/20 ASA firewalls, and Juniper SA 6500 SSL VPN.
The individual provisioned and troubleshooted access issues related to RSA and Entrust (2FA) User Accounts, and performed weekly and monthly backups of all network and security device configurations.

The individual possesses skills in Firewalls & Security, including Cisco ASA, Checkpoint (GAIA, Splat), Palo Alto, Firewall Management (Cisco CSM, Checkpoint SmartCenter, Palo Alto Panorama), RSAEnvision 4.1, IDS, IPS, Qualys, SIEM, Pulse Secure (Juniper SSL VPN), and Cisco Meraki AP.
The individual has skills in Networking & Load Balancing, including F5 LTM, Cisco Routers (29xx, 28xx, 19xx, 3845), Cisco Switches (2960, 3560, 3750E, 4500), LAN, and WAN.

The individual is proficient in Tools & Methodologies such as BMC Remedy, Service-now, ITIL Process (Incident/Change/Problem Management), HLD, LLD, and Programming & Scripting languages like Python and VB Scripting.

Between May 2017 and April 2020, Ashraf Sayed was a Network Security Analyst at Zensar Technologies Egypt in Giza, Egypt, where he managed Checkpoint and Palo Alto firewalls, Cisco routers and switches, and F5 LTM.

From May 2014 to April 2017, Ashraf Sayed was a Network Security Engineer at Infosys Technologies Egypt in Alexandria, Egypt, where he installed, configured, and troubleshooted Checkpoint firewalls, Cisco routers, and switches.
Between May 2011 and April 2014, Ashraf Sayed worked as a Security Operations Analyst at HCL-Comnet Egypt in New Cairo, Egypt, monitoring RSA enVision 4.1 SIEM and analyzing security events.

Egypt, a land steeped in ancient history, also serves as a modern hub for technological expertise, particularly in the realm of network security. Across its diverse cities – from the industrial outskirts of Helwan to the bustling capital of Cairo, the historic port of Alexandria, and the modern district of New Cairo, to Giza – a dedicated cadre of professionals safeguard digital infrastructure. 

This nation is a proving ground for skilled engineers like Ashraf Sayed, a Senior Network Security Analyst at Accenture Egypt, who expertly manages complex systems including Cisco ASA firewalls, F5 LTM, and Pulse Secure. His career, blossoming within Egypt’s tech sector, began in 2010 with hands-on administration of multi-vendor networks in Helwan, mastering Cisco and Juniper platforms. He’s since honed his skills across leading IT services companies – Infosys, HCL-Comnet, Zensar Technologies – each experience deepening his proficiency with firewalls from Checkpoint and Palo Alto, Cisco routing and switching, and security information and event management (SIEM) systems like RSA enVision. 

Egypt, therefore, isn’t merely a geographical location; it’s a dynamic ecosystem where seasoned professionals like Sayed contribute to a growing and increasingly sophisticated cybersecurity landscape, protecting networks and data within its borders and beyond. It’s a place where foundational network knowledge, gained through years of practical experience, translates into advanced security expertise, shaping the future of digital defense.

The Cisco ASA – a stalwart guardian of network perimeters. More than just a firewall, it’s a comprehensive security solution, a digital gatekeeper honed for resilience. This particular iteration, the 5550/20, saw service in Helwan, Egypt, between 2010 and 2011, standing alongside Juniper counterparts in a complex, multi-vendor environment. It’s a device demanding skilled hands; someone capable of not just configuration, but deep troubleshooting and proactive management. 

The ASA isn’t a solitary sentinel, however. It thrives under centralized control, readily managed by systems like Cisco CSM, allowing for unified policy enforcement and monitoring. It’s a key component within a broader security ecosystem, often working in concert with intrusion detection and prevention systems (IDS/IPS), vulnerability scanners like Qualys, and security information and event management (SIEM) platforms. It understands the language of secure access, playing well with technologies like RSA Envision and Pulse Secure VPNs. Though capable on its own, its true strength lies in its integration – a vital link in a chain protecting valuable digital assets. It represents a cornerstone of robust network security, a trusted name in the world of firewalls and security.

Pulse Secure is a critical component in the modern network security landscape, a virtual gatekeeper specializing in secure access solutions. It’s a technology built upon Juniper SSL VPN, offering a robust platform for remote access and application delivery. Though often unseen, Pulse Secure diligently controls who enters the network, verifying identities and enforcing security policies. 

Currently under the watchful eye of professionals like Ashraf Sayed, a Senior Network Security Analyst at Accenture Egypt, Pulse Secure isn’t simply a piece of software – it’s a managed system. Sayed expertly handles its configuration and maintenance alongside other vital security infrastructure like Cisco ASA firewalls and F5 LTM. He ensures its smooth operation, contributing to a secure network environment for Accenture’s clients. 

Pulse Secure stands as a key element in a broader security arsenal, often working in concert with tools like Qualys for vulnerability management and SIEM systems for threat detection. It’s a silent guardian, constantly working to protect valuable data and maintain network integrity.

Cisco Switches are the workhorses of network infrastructure, foundational components within the broader realm of Networking & Load Balancing. They aren’t glamorous, but relentlessly reliable, forming the connective tissue of digital landscapes. Models like the 2960, 3560, 3750E, and the more robust 4500 represent varying levels of capacity and feature sets, each designed to handle different demands within a network. 

These switches aren’t simply hardware; they’re points of control, silently directing the flow of data. Individuals like Ashraf Sayed, a Network Security Analyst and Engineer with experience at Zensar Technologies and Infosys Technologies, understand this intimately. He’s spent years not just *with* these switches, but actively *managing* them – configuring, troubleshooting, and ensuring their smooth operation alongside Cisco routers, firewalls from Checkpoint and Palo Alto, and load balancers like F5 LTM. 

They are the silent partners in maintaining connectivity, the unseen foundation upon which more visible network services are built, and a key area of expertise for those dedicated to keeping the digital world running.

Ashraf Sayed is a seasoned security professional, a watchful guardian of digital borders. He possesses a deep understanding of firewall technologies – Cisco ASA, Checkpoint (GAIA, Splat), and Palo Alto – and is adept at their centralized management using tools like Cisco CSM, Checkpoint SmartCenter, and Palo Alto Panorama. Beyond firewalls, he’s proficient in intrusion detection and prevention systems (IDS/IPS) and vulnerability management with Qualys. His experience extends to the comprehensive view offered by Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) systems, notably RSA Envision 4.1, where, during his tenure as a Security Operations Analyst at HCL-Comnet Egypt (2011-2014), he honed his skills in monitoring and dissecting security events. He’s also familiar with secure remote access solutions like Pulse Secure (Juniper SSL VPN) and wireless security via Cisco Meraki APs. A meticulous investigator, Ashraf doesn’t just react to alerts; he actively *researches* the stories told by network traffic, specifically within the realm of Intrusion Detection Systems, seeking to understand the ‘who, what, and how’ of potential threats. He’s a proactive defender, constantly seeking patterns and anomalies to fortify defenses.

Ashraf Sayed is a seasoned Senior Network Security Analyst currently safeguarding networks at Accenture Egypt in Cairo. Since May 2020, he’s been a key player in maintaining a robust security posture, expertly managing critical infrastructure like Cisco ASA firewalls, F5 LTM, and Pulse Secure, alongside the intricacies of PAC file administration. He doesn’t just *maintain* security; he *designs* it, evidenced by his preparation of detailed design documentation. 

Beyond hands-on expertise, Ashraf possesses a strong foundation in IT service management. He’s fluent in the language of ITIL, particularly adept at navigating Incident, Change, and Problem Management processes. This procedural understanding is coupled with technical skill – he’s proficient in both BMC Remedy and Service-now, the platforms used to enact those processes. 

Ashraf isn’t limited to configuration and troubleshooting. He understands the bigger picture, demonstrated by his familiarity with both High-Level Design (HLD) and Low-Level Design (LLD) documentation. He further complements this with practical coding ability, wielding Python and VB Scripting to automate tasks and enhance security measures. He’s a blend of strategic thinker and pragmatic executor, comfortable both architecting solutions and rolling up his sleeves to implement them.

Ashraf Sayed is a seasoned Senior Network Security Analyst, a digital gatekeeper with over a decade spent fortifying networks against evolving threats. His foundation was laid at Mumbai University, where he earned a Bachelor of Engineering in Information Technology, a period that sparked a dedication to the intricacies of cybersecurity. Since then, his career has unfolded across Egypt’s tech hubs – New Cairo, Alexandria, Giza, and now Cairo – with key roles at HCL-Comnet, Infosys, Zensar Technologies, and currently, Accenture. 

He’s a hands-on professional, equally comfortable diving into the configuration of Cisco ASA firewalls and F5 LTMs as he is analyzing security events within a SIEM like RSA enVision 4.1. His expertise extends to managing Pulse Secure, crafting PAC files, and a deep understanding of both Checkpoint and Palo Alto firewalls, alongside the intricacies of Cisco routers and switches. He doesn’t just maintain security infrastructure; he designs it, evidenced by his preparation of detailed design documents at Accenture. Beyond reactive measures, Ashraf demonstrates a proactive approach, consistently researching traffic patterns within intrusion detection (IDS), intrusion prevention (IPS), and Qualys systems, always seeking to anticipate and neutralize potential vulnerabilities. He maintains an active online presence, readily connectable via email, phone, and professional platforms like LinkedIn and GitHub, showcasing a commitment to staying at the forefront of his field.

Cisco routers are the workhorses of network infrastructure, reliable and versatile devices forming the backbone of countless LANs and WANs. They come in a variety of models – the 29xx, 28xx, and 19xx series representing common deployments, alongside more specialized units like the 3845 – each engineered to direct data flow with precision. These aren’t simply boxes; they’re essential components in the complex choreography of digital communication. 

Experienced network professionals, like Ashraf Sayed, understand their intricacies. He’s spent years not just *with* these routers, but actively shaping networks around them – first at Infosys Technologies Egypt, where he expertly installed and maintained them, and later at Zensar Technologies Egypt, where he managed them alongside critical security infrastructure. They are often found alongside Cisco switches, F5 LTM load balancers, and robust firewall systems like Checkpoint and Palo Alto, forming a layered defense and optimized performance. Cisco routers are a core skill within the broader field of Networking & Load Balancing, a testament to their enduring relevance in a constantly evolving digital landscape. They are the silent architects of connectivity, ensuring data finds its way, reliably and securely.

F5 LTM is a sophisticated and critical component within the landscape of networking and load balancing. It’s a technology consistently entrusted to experienced hands like Ashraf Sayed, a Senior Network Security Analyst at Accenture Egypt, who currently oversees its operation alongside vital security infrastructure like Cisco ASA firewalls and Pulse Secure. Prior to his role at Accenture, Sayed also managed F5 LTM at Zensar Technologies Egypt, demonstrating its enduring value across diverse network environments. It’s a tool demanding expertise, frequently found alongside the management of complex Cisco router and switch configurations – the 29xx, 28xx, 19xx, 3845, 2960, 3560, 3750E, and 4500 series being common companions. Essentially, F5 LTM isn’t a standalone element; it’s a key player in maintaining network availability, performance, and security, requiring a skilled professional to ensure its seamless integration within broader LAN and WAN architectures. Its presence signals a network built for resilience and scalability.

Ashraf Sayed is a seasoned Senior Network Security Analyst currently safeguarding networks at Accenture Egypt in Cairo. Since May 2020, he’s been a key player in maintaining a robust security posture, expertly managing critical infrastructure like Cisco ASA firewalls, F5 LTM, and Pulse Secure VPNs. He’s also adept at configuring and maintaining Proxy Auto-Config (PAC) files. Beyond hands-on operation, Ashraf is a meticulous planner, responsible for crafting detailed design documentation – specifically, Low-Level Designs (LLDs) built upon High-Level Designs (HLDs). His skillset isn’t limited to security devices; he’s fluent in the language of network operations, possessing a strong understanding of ITIL processes – Incident, Change, and Problem Management – and leveraging tools like BMC Remedy and Service-now for efficient workflow. A practical problem-solver, Ashraf complements his operational expertise with scripting capabilities in both Python and VB Scripting, allowing him to automate tasks and tailor solutions to specific needs. He’s a detail-oriented professional bridging the gap between strategic security architecture and its concrete implementation.
